github_security_url_status: direct alert URLs unavailable in findings; SARIF/code-scanning search URLs used

commands: node --check routes/login.js && node --check routes/search.js && node --check lib/insecurity.js && node --check routes/b2bOrder.js && node --check routes/fileUpload.js && node --check routes/profileImageUrlUpload.js && node --check routes/basket.js && node --check server.js
validation_summary.notes: node --check passed. npm run lint failed before linting because installed node_modules is missing acorn. npm test failed before tests because frontend ng is not installed. Runtime uses Node v23.11.0 while package engines declare Node 8-12.

remediations.owasp: A03:2021 Injection; API8:2023 Security Misconfiguration/Injection impact on auth

vulnerable_code.snippet: models.sequelize.query('SELECT * FROM Users WHERE email = \' + (req.body.email || '') + '\' AND password = \' + insecurity.hash(req.body.password || '') + '\' AND deletedAt IS NULL', { model: models.User, plain: true })

changed_code.snippet: models.sequelize.query('SELECT * FROM Users WHERE email = :email AND password = :password AND deletedAt IS NULL', { replacements: { email: req.body.email || '', password: insecurity.hash(req.body.password || '') }, model: models.User, plain: true })

remediations.fix_rationale: Uses Sequelize replacements for attacker-controlled email/password values instead of concatenating input into SQL.
remediations.functionality_preservation: Login query, password hash comparison, OAuth branch, TOTP branch, token issuance, and response shape are preserved; only SQL metacharacter interpretation is removed.

validation.notes: Syntax passed; lint/test unavailable due missing acorn/ng dependencies.

remediations.residual_risk: Stored passwords still use legacy MD5 until PROD-AUDIT-012 migration is designed.

vulnerable_code.snippet: models.sequelize.query('SELECT * FROM Products WHERE ((name LIKE \'%' + criteria + '%\' OR description LIKE \'%' + criteria + '%\') AND deletedAt IS NULL) ORDER BY name')

changed_code.snippet: models.sequelize.query('SELECT * FROM Products WHERE ((name LIKE :criteria OR description LIKE :criteria) AND deletedAt IS NULL) ORDER BY name', { replacements: { criteria: '%' + criteria + '%' } })

remediations.fix_rationale: Binds search criteria as a parameter so UNION/comment syntax remains data inside LIKE predicates.
remediations.functionality_preservation: Public search endpoint, 200-character truncation, LIKE substring matching, ordering, and JSON response are preserved.

validation.notes: Syntax passed; lint/test unavailable due missing acorn/ng dependencies.

remediations.residual_risk: Sequelize advisory-specific dependency upgrade remains separate needs_review/SCA work.

remediations.owasp: A07:2021 Identification and Authentication Failures; A02:2021 Cryptographic Failures

vulnerable_code.snippet: const privateKey = '-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----...-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----'

remediations.fix_rationale: Removing the embedded signing key safely requires secret storage/KMS selection, deployment wiring, and key rotation/revocation planning.
remediations.functionality_preservation: No risky change was made that could invalidate existing tokens or break deployments without a configured signing key.
remediations.residual_risk: JWT signing key remains exposed until migrated and rotated.

remediations.fix_rationale: Pins JWT verification to RS256 and uses the loaded publicKey value directly, preventing HS/RS confusion in the authorization middleware.
remediations.functionality_preservation: Existing RS256 tokens signed by the application continue to validate; non-RS256 tokens are intentionally rejected.
remediations.residual_risk: Dependency upgrades for express-jwt/jsonwebtoken are deferred as SCA developer_decision_required because current package engine/API compatibility needs owner approval.

remediations.owasp: A06:2021 Vulnerable and Outdated Components; A07:2021 Identification and Authentication Failures
remediations.fix_rationale: Safe dependency remediation requires selecting versions compatible with this Node 8-12-era app or approving an engine/runtime uplift and express-jwt API migration.
remediations.functionality_preservation: Avoided a dependency-only branch likely to break JWT middleware without coordinated code/runtime changes.
remediations.residual_risk: Vulnerable JWT packages remain until a compatible upgrade path is approved.

vulnerable_code.snippet: const sandbox = { safeEval, orderLinesData }; vm.createContext(sandbox); vm.runInContext('safeEval(orderLinesData)', sandbox, { timeout: 2000 })

changed_code.snippet: const orderLinesData = body.orderLinesData || '[]'; JSON.parse(orderLinesData); res.json({ cid: body.cid, orderNo: uniqueOrderNumber(), paymentDue: dateTwoWeeksFromNow() })

remediations.fix_rationale: Replaces sandboxed code execution with strict JSON parsing, removing attacker-controlled JavaScript evaluation and notevil reachability.
remediations.functionality_preservation: Valid JSON order payloads still receive cid, orderNo, and paymentDue; empty payloads default to an empty JSON array.
remediations.residual_risk: Invalid JavaScript-like order data is now rejected; clients must send JSON.

remediations.owasp: A01:2021 Broken Access Control; A05:2021 Security Misconfiguration

vulnerable_code.snippet: let absolutePath = path.resolve('uploads/complaints/' + fileName); if (absolutePath.includes(path.resolve('.'))) { entry.pipe(fs.createWriteStream('uploads/complaints/' + fileName)) }

changed_code.snippet: const targetDir = path.resolve('uploads/complaints'); const absolutePath = path.resolve(targetDir, fileName); if (absolutePath === targetDir || absolutePath.indexOf(targetDir + path.sep) !== 0) { entry.autodrain() } else { entry.pipe(fs.createWriteStream(absolutePath)) }

remediations.fix_rationale: Resolves ZIP entries against the complaints directory and rejects paths outside that directory before writing.
remediations.functionality_preservation: Legitimate ZIP entries still extract into uploads/complaints; traversal entries are skipped.
remediations.residual_risk: Archive size/file-count limits are not added in this minimal patch.

vulnerable_code.snippet: vm.runInContext('libxml.parseXml(data, { noblanks: true, noent: true, nocdata: true })', sandbox, { timeout: 2000 }); next(new Error(... + utils.trunc(xmlString, 200) + ...))

changed_code.snippet: const xmlDoc = libxml.parseXml(data, { noblanks: true, noent: false, nocdata: true }); next(new Error('B2B customer complaints via file upload have been deprecated for security reasons (' + file.originalname + ')'))

remediations.fix_rationale: Disables entity expansion and stops reflecting parsed XML content in the error response.
remediations.functionality_preservation: XML complaints remain deprecated with HTTP 410 behavior; file names remain in errors for operator context.
remediations.residual_risk: XML parsing remains present for deprecated flow, but entity expansion is disabled.

changed_code.snippet: isAllowedImageUrl(url, function (err, allowed) { if (err || !allowed) return; request.get({ url, followRedirect: false }).pipe(...) }); DNS-resolved private, loopback, link-local, and credentialed URLs are rejected.

remediations.fix_rationale: Validates scheme, rejects URL credentials, resolves DNS, blocks local/private addresses, and disables redirects before fetching.
remediations.functionality_preservation: Authenticated users can still upload profile images from public HTTP/HTTPS URLs and are redirected to /profile as before.
remediations.residual_risk: No host allowlist or content-type/size validation was added; these may be product policy decisions.

remediations.owasp: A01:2021 Broken Access Control; API1:2023 Broken Object Level Authorization

vulnerable_code.snippet: models.Basket.findOne({ where: { id }, include: [ { model: models.Product, paranoid: false } ] })

changed_code.snippet: const user = insecurity.authenticatedUsers.from(req); if (!user || ... || user.bid != id) return res.status(403).json({ error: 'Basket access denied' }); models.Basket.findOne({ where: { id, userId: user.data.id }, ... })

remediations.fix_rationale: Checks the requested basket id against the authenticated user's basket and filters the query by userId.
remediations.functionality_preservation: Users can retrieve their own basket with the same response shape; cross-user basket access is intentionally denied.
remediations.residual_risk: Other object-level authorization paths should be reviewed separately.

remediations.owasp: A01:2021 Broken Access Control; API5:2023 Broken Function Level Authorization

vulnerable_code.snippet: // app.put('/api/Products/:id', insecurity.isAuthorized()); // = missing function-level access control vulnerability

remediations.fix_rationale: Adds authentication plus admin authorization before generated product PUT handlers can mutate catalog data.
remediations.functionality_preservation: Product GET behavior and authenticated POST behavior are unchanged; product PUT remains available to admins only.
remediations.residual_risk: A broader RBAC policy for all generated CRUD resources is recommended.

remediations.owasp: A01:2021 Broken Access Control; A05:2021 Security Misconfiguration

changed_code.snippet: app.use('/support/logs', insecurity.isAuthorized(), insecurity.isAdmin()); app.use('/support/logs', serveIndex('logs', { 'icons': true, 'view': 'details' }))

remediations.fix_rationale: Requires a valid admin-authenticated request before directory listing or log file serving middleware is reached.
remediations.functionality_preservation: Admin log browsing remains available with the existing serve-index UI; unauthenticated/non-admin access is blocked.
remediations.residual_risk: Logs may still contain sensitive data; redaction/centralized logging should be considered.

remediations.owasp: A02:2021 Cryptographic Failures; A07:2021 Identification and Authentication Failures
remediations.fix_rationale: A safe password hashing fix requires algorithm choice, dependency approval, schema/migration strategy, password reset or rehash-on-login flow, and seeded test-user updates.
remediations.functionality_preservation: Avoided breaking all existing logins by changing hashes without migration data.
remediations.residual_risk: Existing password hashes remain fast unsalted MD5 until migration is approved.

remediations.owasp: A01:2021 Broken Access Control; API3:2023 Broken Object Property Level Authorization
remediations.fix_rationale: Adds an admin authorization check before the generated user listing endpoint can enumerate records.
remediations.functionality_preservation: Authenticated admin users retain list access; regular user registration and individual authorized user GET behavior are unchanged.
remediations.residual_risk: Returned admin-list fields should be minimized through DTOs in a future hardening pass.

remediations.owasp: A01:2021 Broken Access Control / Unvalidated Redirects

changed_code.snippet: const target = new URL(url); const allowedTarget = new URL(allowedUrl); if (target.protocol === allowedTarget.protocol && target.hostname === allowedTarget.hostname && target.pathname === allowedTarget.pathname) return true

remediations.fix_rationale: Parses URLs and compares scheme, hostname, and path exactly instead of trusting substring matches.
remediations.functionality_preservation: Redirects to configured whitelist destinations continue to work; attacker-controlled hosts containing whitelisted text are rejected.
remediations.residual_risk: Query-string matching is intentionally not enforced to preserve destination flexibility.
